boxes.label: algorithm
boxes.text:
Assume all client states S contain a public root dataset D0 (in addition to their private dataset Di
Ui ← FC(data, st, w)
1. g0 ← ModelUpdate(w, D0) // compute update from root dataset, save in client state
2. gi ← ModelUpdate(w, Di) // compute local update from client dataset
3. ui ← gi
Vi ← FP (ui, W)
1. o ←
go
// normalize to unit length
lgoll
2. i ←
Ui
// normalize to unit length
kuil
3. M ← rotation matrix aligning 0 with the x-axis.
4. 0 ← M 0
5. ¯i ← M ¯i // rotate client update by the same angle
6. Represent i and o as θ-bit fixed-point numbers with a designated sign bit, call this
representation g′ and g.
7. If the x-coordinate of gh is negative, submit  as vi.
8. Otherwise, submit a vector of the individual bits of g' as vi. Each coordinate should
be submitted as a sign bit and a binary-encoded magnitude, except for the x-coordinate
which should only have a magnitude since it is non-negative.
W ← FR({vi}i[n], W)
1. Parse vi appropriately as g
2. TSi ← magnitude of x-coordinate of gh // since go is aligned with x-axis
3. Submit g ← ∑i[n] g′ · TSi as global update. Denormalization, rotation, and computa-
tion of global model parameters via w ← w + α · g is performed as post-processing on
the client side
